# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: IAM role for cross-account CI/CD access. Allows assumption by any principal within specified AWS Organization accounts.

Parameters:
  OrganizationId:
    Type: String
    Description: The ID of your AWS Organization (e.g., o-xxxxxxxxxx). This allows CI/CD roles or users from any account within this organization to assume this role.

Resources:
  CiCdCrossAccountRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: CiCdCrossAccountRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            # The '*' principal, when combined with the aws:PrincipalOrgID condition,
            # restricts assumption to any principal (user or role) within the specified organization.
            Principal:
              AWS: '*'
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref OrganizationId
      Description: IAM role allowing CI/CD systems in other accounts within the same AWS Organization to assume it for cross-account operations.
      # Attach policies here to grant specific permissions to the CI/CD system.
      # Example: ManagedPolicyArns:
      #   - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess
      #   - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonS3FullAccess
      # Or define inline policies with the 'Policies' property.
